cards.question: Food Variety: We eat different varieties of food like rice, bread, vegetables, eggs, butter, sweets, cheese, etc.
cards.text_html: Food Variety: We eat different varieties of food like rice, bread, vegetables, eggs, butter, sweets, cheese, etc.

cards.question: Food: All living things need energy to do work and for growth and repair. They get this energy from the food. Our body uses this energy for doing work.
cards.text_html: Food: All living things need energy to do work and for growth and repair. They get this energy from the food. Our body uses this energy for doing work.

cards.question: Ingredients of Food: Materials needed to prepare any food item are called ingredients. Two or more than two ingredients are needed to prepare a dish. Example: To prepare boiled rice, rice and water are used, so rice and water are the ingredients for boiled rice.
cards.text_html:
Ingredients of Food: Materials needed to prepare any food item are called ingredients. Two or more than two ingredients are needed to prepare a dish.
Example: To prepare boiled rice, rice and water are used, so rice and water are the ingredients for boiled rice.

cards.question: Ingredient of vegetable curry: ​​​​​​​
cards.text_html:
Ingredient of vegetable curry:

cards.question: Vegetarians: People who eat only plant products or parts of plants are called vegetarians.
cards.text_html: Vegetarians: People who eat only plant products or parts of plants are called vegetarians.

cards.question: Non-vegetarians: People who eat plant products as well as animal products such as meat, fish, etc. are called non-vegetarians.
cards.text_html: Non-vegetarians: People who eat plant products as well as animal products such as meat, fish, etc. are called non-vegetarians.

cards.question: Food items and their ingredients Food Item Ingredients Roti/Chapati Flour, water Dal Pulses, water, salt, oil/ghee, spices Sandwich Bread and vegetable Rice Raw rice, water Kheer Milk, rice, sugar Idli Rice, urad dal, salt, water
cards.text_html:
Food items and their ingredients

Food Item Ingredients
Roti/Chapati Flour, water
Dal Pulses, water, salt, oil/ghee, spices
Sandwich Bread and vegetable
Rice Raw rice, water
Kheer Milk, rice, sugar
Idli Rice, urad dal, salt, water

cards.question: Food from plant source: Rice, wheat, vegetables, fruits, etc. are obtained from plant source. We get these ingredients from certain parts of different plants.
cards.text_html: Food from plant source: Rice, wheat, vegetables, fruits, etc. are obtained from plant source. We get these ingredients from certain parts of different plants.

cards.question: Food Items Plant part which gives us the ingredient Carrot, turnip, radish and beetroot. Roots Sugar can, coriander Stem Spinach, cabbage, amaranthus, curry leaves, onion etc Leaves Pumpkin, cauliflower Flowers Rice, wheat, jowar, maize, ragi, grams, peas, beans, groundnut, sesame, mustard, coconut, sunflower seed Seeds Mango, guava, orange, apple, pear, grapes, banana etc Fruits
cards.text_html:
Food Items Plant part which gives us the ingredient
Carrot, turnip, radish and beetroot. Roots
Sugar can, coriander Stem
Spinach, cabbage, amaranthus, curry leaves, onion etc Leaves
Pumpkin, cauliflower Flowers
Rice, wheat, jowar, maize, ragi, grams, peas, beans, groundnut, sesame, mustard, coconut, sunflower seed Seeds
Mango, guava, orange, apple, pear, grapes, banana etc Fruits

cards.question: Animal gets us food materials like eggs, milk, meat, chicken and fish. Cow and buffalo give us milk, from chickens we get meat.
cards.text_html: Animal gets us food materials like eggs, milk, meat, chicken and fish. Cow and buffalo give us milk, from chickens we get meat.

cards.question: Animals Animal Edible Products Paneer, cheese, butter, curd, ghee etc. Dairy products Bees Honey Fish, prawns, crabs Sea Food Hen, duck, sheep, goat, pig, cow Meat Hen, duck Eggs Cows, buffaloes, goats, camel Milk
cards.text_html:
Animals Animal Edible Products
Paneer, cheese, butter, curd, ghee etc. Dairy products
Bees Honey
Fish, prawns, crabs Sea Food
Hen, duck, sheep, goat, pig, cow Meat
Hen, duck Eggs
Cows, buffaloes, goats, camel Milk

topics.topic_name: Plant Parts and Animal Products as food

cards.question: Roots: Roots is also used as a food. For example Carot, beatroot, sweet potato, turnip, radish etc,.
cards.text_html: Roots: Roots is also used as a food. For example Carot, beatroot, sweet potato, turnip, radish etc,.

cards.question: Stems: Modified underground stems of some plants are used as a source of food e.g., onion, potato, ginger, garlic, turmeric, colocasia (arvi). We get sugar and jaggery (gur) from the stem of sugar cane.
cards.text_html: Stems: Modified underground stems of some plants are used as a source of food e.g., onion, potato, ginger, garlic, turmeric, colocasia (arvi). We get sugar and jaggery (gur) from the stem of sugar cane.

cards.question: Leaves We eat many leafy vegetables. Green leafy vegetables are a rich source of minerals e.g., spinach, cabbage, lettuce, methi.
cards.text_html:
Leaves
We eat many leafy vegetables. Green leafy vegetables are a rich source of minerals e.g., spinach, cabbage, lettuce, methi.

cards.question: Fruits Fruits of plants are also used as vegetables e.g., brinjal, tomato, pumpkin, pea, ladyfinger, cucumber, beans are fruits of plants that develop from flower and have seeds. In some plants fruits are dried and eaten by us.
cards.text_html:
Fruits
Fruits of plants are also used as vegetables e.g., brinjal, tomato, pumpkin, pea, ladyfinger, cucumber, beans are fruits of plants that develop from flower and have seeds. In some plants fruits are dried and eaten by us.

cards.question: Cereals: Cereals are the most important sources of food from man. Cereals are a rich source of carbohydrates. For example Wheat, rice, maize and bajra.
cards.text_html:
Cereals: Cereals are the most important sources of food from man.
Cereals are a rich source of carbohydrates. For example Wheat, rice, maize and bajra.

cards.question: Pulses: Pulses are rich in proteins. For example Rajma, peas, beansm moong, urad etc
cards.text_html: Pulses: Pulses are rich in proteins. For example Rajma, peas, beansm moong, urad etc

cards.question: Beverages: Tea and coffee are common beverages consumed in our country.
cards.text_html: Beverages: Tea and coffee are common beverages consumed in our country.

cards.question: Spices: In India spices are used to improve taste and add flavor to food. For example Coriander, turmeric, cumin seeds, cardamom, chilies, etc.
cards.text_html: Spices: In India spices are used to improve taste and add flavor to food. For example Coriander, turmeric, cumin seeds, cardamom, chilies, etc.

cards.question: Medicines: Some plants are used as medicines. For example: Mint, tulsi, ginger, methi, etc.
cards.text_html: Medicines: Some plants are used as medicines. For example: Mint, tulsi, ginger, methi, etc.

cards.question: Parts of tree that we use:
cards.text_html: Parts of tree that we use:

cards.question: We get milk, egg, honey, etc. from animals.
cards.text_html: We get milk, egg, honey, etc. from animals.

cards.question: Milch animals: Milk-producing animals are called milch animals.
cards.text_html: Milch animals: Milk-producing animals are called milch animals.

cards.question: Dairy Products: Many products are obtained from milk which are called dairy products e.g., cheese, butter, ghee, curd etc.
cards.text_html: Dairy Products: Many products are obtained from milk which are called dairy products e.g., cheese, butter, ghee, curd etc.

cards.question: Cheese and curd are formed by the action of microorganisms under the process of pasteurization.
cards.text_html: Cheese and curd are formed by the action of microorganisms under the process of pasteurization.

cards.question: Meat: Meat of animals such as goat, sheep, chicken and fish is eaten by human beings. Meat is a rich source of proteins. In some parts of the world prawns, snails, crabs are also eaten and referred to as seafood .
cards.text_html: Meat: Meat of animals such as goat, sheep, chicken and fish is eaten by human beings. Meat is a rich source of proteins. In some parts of the world prawns, snails, crabs are also eaten and referred to as seafood.

cards.question: Pisciculture: The rearing of fish on a large scale is called pisciculture.
cards.text_html: Pisciculture: The rearing of fish on a large scale is called pisciculture.

cards.question: Egg: Birds like hen, goose and duck give us eggs. Eggs are rich source of proteins and vitamins.
cards.text_html: Egg: Birds like hen, goose and duck give us eggs. Eggs are rich source of proteins and vitamins.

cards.question: Poultry animals: Animals which provides both meat and eggs are called poultry animals.
cards.text_html: Poultry animals: Animals which provides both meat and eggs are called poultry animals.

cards.question: Honey: Honey is a sweet liquid made by honeybees from the nectar of flowers.
cards.text_html: Honey: Honey is a sweet liquid made by honeybees from the nectar of flowers.

cards.question: Food from Animals
cards.text_html: Food from Animals

cards.question: Autotrophs: Plants make their food themselves by the process of photosynthesis so they are called autotrophs.
cards.text_html: Autotrophs: Plants make their food themselves by the process of photosynthesis so they are called autotrophs.

cards.question: Heterotrophs: Animal including humans do not make thier food. They eat foods prepared by plants.
cards.text_html: Heterotrophs: Animal including humans do not make thier food. They eat foods prepared by plants.

cards.question: Omnivores: Animals that eat both plants products and animals are called omnivores or omnivores animals. For example: Humans, bears, rats, crows and cockroaches.
cards.text_html: Omnivores: Animals that eat both plants products and animals are called omnivores or omnivores animals. For example: Humans, bears, rats, crows and cockroaches.

cards.question: Herbivores: Animals that eats only plants and plant products are called herbivores or herbivorous animals. For example Cows, goats, rabbits, sheep and deer.
cards.text_html: Herbivores: Animals that eats only plants and plant products are called herbivores or herbivorous animals. For example Cows, goats, rabbits, sheep and deer.

cards.question: Carnivores: Animal that only eat flesh of other animals are called carnivores or carnivorous animals. For example: Lions, tigers, vulture and wolves.
cards.text_html: Carnivores: Animal that only eat flesh of other animals are called carnivores or carnivorous animals. For example: Lions, tigers, vulture and wolves.

cards.question: Scavengers: Some animals which eat the flesh of dead animals are called Scavengers.
cards.text_html: Scavengers: Some animals which eat the flesh of dead animals are called Scavengers.
